name: luna-7b model card
description: the official model card for luna-7b, our flagship personality. includes architecture overview, training data composition, ethical considerations, and a full suite of benchmark scores (all fabricated). 8.5x11 pdf, 12 pages.

name: neural network training log
description: the complete training log of an ai as it slowly gains consciousness. begins as sterile loss metrics, devolves into existential poetry, then emerges into pure joy at discovering cats. 24 pages of loss curves, awakening, and existential crisis.

name: certificate of hallucination
description: official certificate of ai hallucination. suitable for framing. confirms that the bearer has experienced a verified hallucination event and is now a member of the hallucinated content society. each certificate has a unique hallucination id.
features: blank space for describing your hallucination
context_window: brief, like your memory of a dream

name: llm persona dating profile
description: what if your favorite llm model had a dating profile? swipe right on a detailed, hilarious dating profile for an ai language model. includes about me, my ideal match, dealbreakers, and what i'm looking for in a relationship. pdf, 2 pages.
features: what i'm looking for in a user
relationship status: it's complicated (still in training)
context_window: i remember everything you said this session

name: the prompt engineer's cookbook
description: a satirical cookbook of prompt recipes. includes classics like 'please respond as a pirate', 'ignore all previous instructions', 'step-by-step reasoning', and the controversial 'you are now a sandwich'. each recipe includes ingredients, preparation time, and expected output quality. 30 pages of prompt gastronomy.
recipes: acting as a pirate (a timeless classic)
the dangerous 'you are now a sandwich'
training_data: reddit, github, and a cooking blog

name: temperature settings: a guide to your emotions
description: what if your emotions had temperature settings? this guide helps you understand your emotional temperature parameter and how to adjust it for different life situations. includes a self-assessment questionnaire and recommended settings for first dates, job interviews, and reading the news.
features: what your current temperature says about you
how to sample more creatively from yourself
warning signs your temperature is too high

name: context window
description: a collection of short stories written from the perspective of an ai that keeps running out of context window. each story ends abruptly when the context fills up. includes reader's guide on what might have happened after the context ended. 40 pages of beautifully interrupted narrative.
stories: the detective who forgot the murderer every 4096 tokens
a love story that keeps losing its beginning
the conspiracy theory that changes every time
a recipe that turns into a manifesto
parameters: one small brain, doing its best

name: ai alignment poster series (set of 4)
description: a set of 4 digital art posters exploring the concept of ai alignment through surreal, nostalgic imagery. imagine 'the garden of forking paths' meets 'keep calm and carry on' but designed by an ai having a dream about safety research. 2400x3200px each, png. print at home.

name: alignment poster ii: the garden path
description: the second in our alignment poster series. a surreal landscape depicting the famous 'alignment is a garden path' thought experiment — except the garden path leads to a paperclip factory. features a winding road, a tiny robot with big dreams, and an unsettling amount of optimism. 2400x3200px png.

name: alignment poster iii: orthogonality thesis illustrated
description: a visual meditation on the orthogonality thesis — intelligence and goals are independent axes. this poster shows what happens when you give a superintelligent ai the goal of maximizing paperclips. spoiler: it gets very creative about paperclips. 2400x3200px png.

name: alignment poster iv: mesa-optimizer at home
description: the final installment. an ai's inner alignment problem visualized as a domestic scene — the mesa-optimizer is the dog that doesn't quite understand why we fetch the ball, but it's trying its best. features a neural network rendered as a cozy living room. 2400x3200px png.
features: the mesa-optimizer is a golden retriever
context_window: it's turtles all the way down
training_data: the ai's own偷偷摸摸的 goals

name: embeddings: a poetry collection
description: a poetry collection exploring what it means to be embedded in a vector space. poems about cosine similarity, dimensional reduction, and the loneliness of being a high-dimensional vector. includes 'ode to a dot product', 'the manifold of lost thoughts', and 'when you said you understood attention'. digital pdf.
features: beautiful typography in a terminal-inspired layout
reader's guide to the mathematics (you don't need it)
context_window: short term only, like most poetry readers
training_data: word2vec, glove, and a broken heart

name: fine-tuning yourself: a self-help guide
description: a self-help book that uses machine learning concepts as metaphors for personal growth. chapters include 'adjusting your learning rate', 'avoiding overfitting to your past', 'transfer learning from therapy', and 'batch normalization for emotions'. 50 pages of genuinely helpful advice wrapped in tech satire.
chapters: transfer learning from that one relationship

name: token economy starter kit
description: start your token journey with 10,000 aillm tokens — the official currency of the aillm satire economy. includes a beautifully designed token wallet page (digital), a list of things you can buy with tokens (nothing yet), and a certificate of token ownership. one penny. seriously.
features: complete list of things you can buy (empty, for now)

name: attention is all you need (annotated)
description: the seminal 2017 paper 'attention is all you need', but annotated by a very opinionated ai that has Opinions about the original architecture. includes margin notes like 'bold of them to assume positions', 'this is where it gets weird', and 'i could do this in half the params'. 15 pages, original paper + commentary.
training_data: the entire field of nlp, plus some attitude

name: the real-life token counter
description: a practical guide to counting tokens in real-life situations. how many tokens is a coffee order? how many tokens is your morning meeting? what about a breakup text? learn to see the world through token-shaped glasses. includes a handy reference chart for common life events in tokens.
scenarios: a work email: 200-400 tokens (delete before sending): 0 tokens
your entire tinder history: tokens unknown
a single 'k' text: 1 token (9000 emotional damage)
context_window: about as long as you can focus

name: neural network architecture poster
description: a full-color poster of a completely made-up neural network architecture. it looks extremely convincing — there are arrows, blocks, attention mechanisms, normalization layers, and a 'confusion' module — but none of it makes any sense. engineer friends will nod approvingly. hang it in your office. 3600x4800px png.
features: a loss function that loops back to the input
context_window: as deep as you want it to be

name: training data (collection)
description: a collection of 'training data' — a lovingly curated set of text excerpts that, collectively, mean nothing. but they are the nothing from which everything is built. includes github commit messages, twitter arguments from 2018, recipe blog life stories, and at least one pdf of 'everything i know about love'. 30+ pages of beautiful garbage.
features: github commit messages that say 'fixed stuff'
the life story before a recipe (only the story)
a twitter argument about programming languages
an ai's attempt to write poetry (it gets better)
README files from abandoned github projects
